title: Neri — Japanese formation aid from tororo aoi
materials: dried tororo aoi root (Abelmoschus manihot, Japanese hibiscus) OR formation-aid-peo as a synthetic substitute
cold water (do not heat tororo aoi — heat degrades the mucilage)
clean muslin or cheesecloth for straining
sources: Dard Hunter, Old Papermaking (1923) — public domain. Formation aid preparation in Japanese washi documented in Timothy Barrett, Japanese Papermaking: Traditions, Tools and Techniques (1983, copyright, structural description only).
notes: Neri is the viscous formation aid used in nagashizuki (Japanese sheet forming). It dramatically slows vat drainage, allowing the long-fibre suspension to be poured and tossed on the mould rather than scooped. Without neri, long-fibre washi pulp drains too quickly and produces uneven sheets. Covers: preparing tororo aoi from dried root (soak in cold water for several hours, squeeze the root to release mucilage, strain), the correct neri concentration (vat water should feel slightly slippery but not gel-like), PEO (polyethylene oxide) as a reliable synthetic alternative, how much to add to the vat (start with less than you think), signs of over-neri (pulp won't drain). Cross-reference nagashizuki sheet forming.
